title: Listing containers fails with "container xxx does not exist in database: no such container"
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
I encountered the same bug as described in #418 using the podman API

>could not list containers: Error response from daemon: container bf4677025fecfa5c4f0883537d857d76a613a5ca7c7c9d79b81c1b7ec2dc277a does not exist in database: no such container

I'm using the go docker API library and get the error during tests that create and delete containers concurrently.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

This test reliably provokes the error
```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"strconv"
	"testing"

	"github.com/docker/docker/api/types"
	"github.com/docker/docker/api/types/container"
	"github.com/docker/docker/client"
)

func Test_ProvokeErr(t *testing.T) {
	podClient, err := client.NewEnvClient()
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatal(err)
	}
	for i := 0; i < 50; i++ {
		t.Run(strconv.Itoa(i), func(t *testing.T) {
			t.Parallel()
			creationResp, err := podClient.ContainerCreate(
				context.Background(),
				&container.Config{Image: "alpine"},
				nil, nil, "",
			)
			if err != nil {
				t.Fatal(err)
			}

			_, err = podClient.ContainerList(context.Background(), types.ContainerListOptions{All: true})
			if err != nil {
				t.Error("could not list containers:", err)
			}

			err = podClient.ContainerRemove(context.Background(), creationResp.ID, types.ContainerRemoveOptions{})
			if err != nil {
				t.Error(err)
			}
		})
	}
}
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```log
test_test.go:32: could not list containers: Error response from daemon: container edb1f86b964f08558aa2ba4799aa81db59c2fe3ff9754c704252db2df708682e does not exist in database: no such container
test_test.go:32: could not list containers: Error response from daemon: container c0114fc1f0a10a809db3a681fbd0d612aa9e87b4170a08f5a9b028ec442e33c1 does not exist in database: no such container
...
```
**Describe the results you expected:**
no errors

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.1.0
API Version:  3.1.0
Go Version:   go1.16
Built:        Tue Mar 30 15:29:36 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

<details>
  <summary>Package info</summary>

```
Name         : podman
Epoch        : 2
Version      : 3.1.0
Release      : 1.fc34
Architecture : x86_64
Size         : 46 M
Source       : podman-3.1.0-1.fc34.src.rpm
Repository   : @System
From repo    : fedora
Summary      : Manage Pods, Containers and Container Images
URL          : https://podman.io/
License      : ASL 2.0
Description  : podman (Pod Manager) is a fully featured container engine that is a simple
             : daemonless tool.  podman provides a Docker-CLI comparable command line that
             : eases the transition from other container engines and allows the management of
             : pods, containers and images.  Simply put: alias docker=podman.
             : Most podman commands can be run as a regular user, without requiring
             : additional privileges.
             : 
             : podman uses Buildah(1) internally to create container images.
             : Both tools share image (not container) storage, hence each can use or
             : manipulate images (but not containers) created by the other.
             : 
             : Manage Pods, Containers and Container Images
             : podman Simple management tool for pods, containers and images
```
</details>

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes
